name: ADVANCE AUTO PARTS INC
business: Advance Auto Parts is a leading automotive aftermarket parts provider in North America, serving professional installers and do-it-yourself (DIY) customers. It offers a broad selection of brand-name, OEM, and owned-brand automotive replacement parts, accessories, batteries, and maintenance items for domestic and imported cars, vans, SUVs, and light and heavy-duty trucks. As of January 3, 2026, the company operated 4,305 stores and served 809 independently owned Carquest branded stores.

exposures.sentence: Some of the Company's brands include Bosch, Castrol, Dayco, Denso, Gates, Meguiar's TM, Mobil 1 TM, Moog, Monroe, NGK, Prestone, Purolator and Wagner.
The Company also serves customers online at www.AdvanceAutoParts.com or on the Advance Mobile App.
The Company is executing supply chain initiatives to drive efficiency, improve inventory availability and establish a scalable foundation to support merchandising precision and profitable growth.
the Company is currently using and intends to use innovative technologies, including artificial intelligence, in its business.

In the event of a security breach or other cyber security incident, the Company could experience adverse operational effects or interruptions and/or become subject to legal or regulatory proceedings, any of which could result in substantial costs and damage to its reputation in the marketplace.

catalysts.event: Launch of new oil and fluids brand ARGOS
catalysts.sentence: our new oil and fluids brand, ARGOS, launched in January 2026, designed to meet growing consumer demand for high-quality, affordable auto care products.

catalysts.event: Anticipated capital expenditures of approximately $300 million in fiscal 2026
catalysts.sentence: In 2026, the Company currently anticipates that the Company's capital expenditures related to such investments will be approximately $300 million, reflecting continued investment in store infrastructure upgrades and the growth of our store and market hub footprint, but this amount may vary depending on business conditions and other factors.

The Company estimates that it will incur additional expenses of approximately $30 million to $40 million through fiscal 2026, substantially all of which is expected to be cash expenses, primarily composed of lease and termination costs associated with closed stores and closed distribution center leases.
